entry.title: Washington Post: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: U.S. attorney general visits Kyiv
  • Two Jesuit priests killed in Mexican area torn by drug violence
  • Netanyahu prepares for a comeback in Israel’s next elections
  • Britain’s trains grind to a halt in biggest rail strike in 30 years
  • Deadly South Asia floods leave families trapped without food and water
  • Russia threatens Lithuania for enforcing E.U. sanctions on Kaliningrad
  • Sunrise rituals and music parties: The world marks the summer solstice
  • American detainees won’t get Geneva Convention protections, Kremlin says
  • Russia’s Muratov sells Nobel medal for $103.5M to help Ukrainian children
  • Canada banning single-use plastics to combat pollution, climate change
  • Iraq’s wildcard cleric upends politics as summer’s heat descends
  • In Venezuela, priests convicted of abuse have returned to ministry
  • As businesses flee Russia, a New York model returns to Moscow
  • World’s heaviest freshwater fish, a 661-pound stingray, caught in Cambodia
  • How Kaliningrad, Russian land ringed by NATO, is tangled in Ukraine war
  • Kremlin says captured Americans are ‘soldiers of fortune,’ not subject to Geneva Conventions
  • Japan has always been refugee-averse. Then Ukraine happened.
  • Ben Stiller tells Zelensky in Ukraine: ‘You’re my hero’
  • George Lamming, renowned Caribbean novelist, dies at 94
  • As Latin America embraces a new left, the U.S. could take a back seat
  • Jesuits: 2 priests killed in northern Mexico
  • Lapid, set to be Israel's next premier, faces critical test
  • Palestinians say man stabbed to death by Israeli settler
  • Saudi crown prince concludes Egypt visit, heads to Jordan
  • Polish man who rescued Jews during the Holocaust dies at 102
  • A boy died in hot car as his mom prepared his sister’s birthday party
  • Police response in Uvalde was ‘an abject failure,’ Texas DPS chief says
  • Updated timeline: How police responded to the Texas school shooter
  • Clela Rorex, ‘unsung hero’ of marriage equality movement, dies at 78
  • Jan. 6 hearing live updates: Panel hearing testimony on Trump efforts to pressure state officials
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Kellogg will split into 3 companies as it leans into snacks
  • Crypto’s frozen mystery: The fate of billions in Celsius deposits
  • Relief rally lifts Dow 500 points despite rising recession fears
  • Supreme Court rejects appeal from Roundup maker over cancer claims
  • Activision Blizzard shareholders vote for public harassment report
  • NASA finally fuels moon rocket, but first launch remains a question
  • Will Elizabeth Holmes’s former business partner face the same fate?
  • Luxury touches with D.C.’s bustling Kennedy Street around the corner
  • Cordless power tools are the rage but may cost you more in the end
  • Remote work continues to shift U.S. housing landscape, Zillow finds
  • Does Mike Pence Still Have a Chance in 2024?
  • Fed Pays Too Much Heed to 500 Random Opinions
  • Why This Bear Market Is Bad for Stocks and Bonds
  • The Supreme Court Has Just Eroded First Amendment Law
  • How Chip Neutrality Scuppered Nvidia Deal to Buy Arm: QuickTake
  • How a Battery Metals Squeeze Puts EV Future at Risk
  • Why China and US Disagree on Forced Labor in Xinjiang
  • How China’s Property Developers Got Into Such a Mess: QuickTake
  • From ‘pariah’ to ‘move past it’: How Biden set aside press freedom
  • The GOP must make it clear: Eric Greitens’s ad is out of bounds
  • The courts still haven’t figured out how to reconcile science with law
  • The Pentagon gets more money, and Americans pay the price
  • Republicans can’t celebrate ‘Top Gun: Maverick’ while denigrating the military
  • Robin Thede on comedically reflecting on Black America through ‘A Black Lady Sketch Show’
  • Gunning for RINOs: A Republican hits a new low
  • The FDA is taking a historic step to reduce smoking deaths. It’s overdue.
  • Admitting more refugees from Latin America is a responsible step forward
  • Expectations about prosecuting Trump may be shifting
  • Trump, CNN and ‘the big lie’
  • Trump’s open confession will loom large at today’s Jan. 6 hearing
  • The killings of two men in the Amazon tell a larger story
  • We should not promote criminal violence as a viable form of protest
  • What the U.S. can do to mitigate global air pollution
  • A potential lesson in critical real estate theory
  • Clearing up Susan Collins’s record
  • What the court’s ‘originalists’ can do about guns
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 21 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-21 19:00PM.
entry.content:

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 21 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-21 18:18PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong maritime authorities request report from owner after Jumbo Floating Restaurant sinks in South China Sea, reveal attraction was headed for Cambodia
  • 20,000 Hong Kong households facing 2-day blackout after Yuen Long cable fire triggers power outages, MTR suspension and disrupted hospital services
  • 76 per cent of young people polled identify as ‘Hongkongers’, while only 2 per cent think of themselves as ‘Chinese’
  • Häagen-Dazs Hong Kong apologises after banned pesticide found in 2 types of vanilla ice cream products
  • Hong Kong protests: 2 teenagers sentenced to correctional training, hard labour for vandalising bakery during 2019 protests
  • President Xi Jinping sends Hong Kong personal message ahead of 25th anniversary handover celebrations
  • Hong Kong and BN(O) passport holders can travel to South Korea visa-free again after restrictions eased, while city logs 1,198 Covid cases
  • Will NFT, metaverse developments in Hong Kong help deliver the city’s next ‘Octopus moment’ in a post-pandemic world?
  • Before the storm (2007-2012): Hong Kong’s national pride and a rare political deal in 25 photos
  • Former Hong Kong leader CY Leung says American Express apologised to wife Regina after suing her over allegedly unpaid HK$93,155 credit card bill
  • Satire in Hong Kong: ‘You have to laugh, or you will cry,’ says political cartoonist Harry Harrison
  • Anti-terrorism campaign urges Hong Kong hardware store owners, chemical suppliers to report suspicious customers
  • Beijing sends 29 warplanes into Taiwan’s air defence zone in one of largest fly-bys of 2022
  • China plans to review every single social media comment, sparking more censorship fears
  • Trade war: China, US urged to ‘start small’ as Biden ponders Trump-era tariff rollback amid record inflation
  • In Brussels, tram ads for 25th Hong Kong handover anniversary pulled after complaints
  • Uygur Forced Labour Prevention Act takes effect in US, banning imports from Xinjiang
  • Police officer fired and 5 under investigation over attack in women in Chinese city of Tangshan
  • Chinese warships appear to be sailing around Japan amid tensions
  • North Korean imports of Chinese soybeans surge as coronavirus outbreak ramps up food shortage fears
  • Chinese province offers more maternity leave to help avert population crisis
  • Qatar World Cup organisers reverse course on Taiwan name change
  • Does China’s demotion of its deputy foreign minister signal a rethink over Russia ties?
  • China’s southern provinces on alert as flooding breaks records while heat hits further north
  • Tencent’s WeChat bans public accounts from providing secondary trading services for NFTs
  • ‘We will not give up’: wife of jailed Chinese coal magnate vows to push for new trial after he loses appeal
  • COP15: China gives up host role with call for urgent meeting on biodiversity, UN says
  • Tencent trims stake in Koolearn, New Oriental’s live-streaming unit, as new venture fuels a six-fold jump in stock
  • EU companies in China pay the price for Brussels stance on Xinjiang
  • NetEase to launch app of virtual meeting platform Yaotai in August, as competition heats up on metaverse-related products
  • Teen girl in China steals US$18,000 meant for sick mother’s operation to go on a shopping spree
  • China’s political party school in Africa takes first students from 6 countries
  • China’s Horn of Africa envoy tells regional peace conference he is ready to mediate disputes
  • European nations tell China ‘self-isolation’ is stoking ‘mistrust’ in plea for easing of Covid-19 curbs
  • Syed Saddiq faced ‘pressure’ by Malaysia’s PPBM, his parents say during corruption trial
  • South Korea successfully launches home-grown Nuri space rocket after failed attempt last year
  • Singapore unveils US$1 billion package as cost of living rises amid inflation, Ukraine war
  • Bangladesh, India scramble to help 9 million people hit by ‘worst floods in 122 years’
  • As BTS ‘takes a break’, what does it mean for the K-pop supergroup’s future?
  • Singapore seeks ‘online safety’ rules to remove or block harmful social media content
  • Coronavirus: Is North Korea getting ready to declare victory over Covid-19?
  • A new Quad? Japan, South Korea, Australia, New Zealand eye four-way anti-China summit on sidelines of Nato meet
  • Indonesian Islamist group Khilafatul Muslimin with ‘hundreds of thousands’ of members wants to build caliphate
  • Top French court rules against burkinis in city pool
  • Ukraine war: Russia vows to inflict pain on Lithuania over Kaliningrad goods ‘blockade’
  • Ukraine war: Russia says captured US fighters may face death penalty
  • UK grinds to halt as biggest rail strike in 30 years strands thousands of commuters
  • Inside Ukraine’s secret, helicopter rescue missions: ‘We lost a lot of pilots’
  • Russia’s Putin alert for signs of eroding Western unity on Ukraine
  • Belgium returns Congo independence hero Patrice Lumumba’s tooth to family
  • Elon Musk’s 18-year-old trans child seeks name change to sever ties with father
  • As Russia closes its doors on academics, Central Asian universities have an opportunity to expand their networks
  • Despite Western weapons, Ukraine is outgunned in the east
  • Ukraine war: US and allies mulling Russian oil price cap, Treasury chief Janet Yellen says
  • Ukraine war: Russia says captured US fighters must be ‘held responsible for their crimes’
  • Hopes fade for swift Nato accession for Finland and Sweden
  • Ukraine conflict highlights the need to find better ways to feed the world
  • Turmoil in global market for blockchain-based DeFi products leaves investors holding the bag
  • Chinese EV start-up Li Auto launches L9, the ‘full-size SUV with no match on the market’
  • As it turns 22, CEO says HKEX must learn from tech firms to deliver new products and services at low cost
  • Didi apps ousted from China app stores for privacy reasons near return after ride-hailing firm’s NYSE delisting, sources say
  • Evergrande: defaulted developer gets delisting reminder as restructuring, probe on US$2 billion deposits leave investors in the dark
  • China’s climate policies are forcing renewable energy developers to incorporate costly storage facilities into new solar and wind projects
  • US Federal Reserve’s attempt to turn inflation tide leaves Asian economies exposed
  • Buying UK property: here’s a tactic to get faster mortgage loan approval to fund your purchase
  • Hong Kong stocks log longest winning streak in three weeks on China recovery optimism as property and casino stocks rally
  • Kowloon may attract more Chinese firms as office-rental cost gap narrows between Greater Bay Area cities and Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong developers lower prices to generate sales amid interest rate hikes, high supply
  • New Zealand’s housing reforms lead the way on tackling affordability and speculation
  • China’s national ambitions have become untethered from personal aspirations in the name of zero Covid
  • The Karate Kid musical’s Mr Miyagi on Asian representation, how it’s ‘not a movie being staged’, and stepping into Pat Morita’s shoes
  • Hong Kong Philharmonic thrill with vibrant Respighi and a pyrotechnic piccolo premiere
  • Documenta 2022: curated by Asians for first time, German art event’s highs and lows as opening week kicks off
  • Broker: Hirokazu Kore-eda, acclaimed Japanese director, on his first Korean film, starring Song Kang-ho, Gang Dong-won and IU
  • Netflix K-drama Alchemy of Souls: Jung So-min, Lee Jae-wook in CGI-heavy fantasy action romance that offers a magical mix of genres
  • Why is mead making a comeback? The craft brewers in Hong Kong reviving ancient alcoholic beverage for the modern drinker
  • Mamma Mia! ABBA’s avatar concerts highlight change as performing arts innovation connects with audiences
  • When Hong Kong seamen returned from the Falklands war in 1982, including survivors of air attacks on their ships
  • Why use of social media to market fashion in China and West is so different – the hard sell from influencers doesn’t work in US or Europe … yet
  • Taboo: how the word came to describe topics such as menstruation and race, and how it helped one tourist hotspot achieve zero Covid
  • What do we see when dying? How a psychedelic trip can trigger at life’s end, and why we need to be better prepared for it
  • Blue Ivy Carter and Harper Beckham at age 10: how do their childhoods compare? Besides famous parents Beyoncé and Jay-Z, and Posh and Becks, they are both budding fashionistas born into major wealth
  • Who is Jennifer Lopez’s gender-neutral teen, Emme Muñiz? The young singer uses they/them pronouns, has written a children’s book, and is close with twin Max and Ben Affleck’s kids Seraphina and Samuel
  • Takashi Murakami’s NFT apology to fans: launching just before the 2022 crypto market crash, the Japanese contemporary artist saw his Murakami Flowers’ value drop from US$260,000 to a mere US$2,000
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Prada blended grunge with nostalgia for spring/summer 2023, with Miuccia Prada and Raf Simons creating suits, sleeveless short sets and trenches for the season
  • Inside Bollywood legend Jackie Shroff’s US$4 million Mumbai family home: he lives in a luxurious eight-bedroom flat with Tiger, Krishna and Ayesha – and counts Rani Mukerji as a neighbour
  • Inside tech billionaire Larry Ellison’s US$160 million superyacht Musashi: the Oracle co-founder’s 88-metre, Japanese-style vessel was even featured on Netflix’s The Dropout
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
